prestazioni: Replace the original XPAG 1,250cc inline-4 engine (engine code: XPAG) with a fully built XPAG 1,466cc stroker kit from MG Owners Club (MGO), featuring forged Mahle pistons (83mm bore), Cunningham forged steel crankshaft (89mm stroke), and steel connecting rods with ARP rod bolts. Pair with a custom billet aluminum cylinder head by Peter Burgess Racing, ported and polished for improved airflow, with oversized 1.5-inch SU carburetors and a custom intake manifold for optimized fuel delivery.

Install a bespoke 4-into-1 stainless steel exhaust system by Janspeed Engineering, designed specifically for the TA chassis with 1.75-inch primary tubes and a 2.5-inch collector, terminating in a single 3-inch polished tip. Pair with a custom 4-2-1 header by MG Metro specialist John Chatham, ensuring optimal scavenging and torque delivery for the XPAG engine.

Upgrade the drivetrain with a close-ratio 4-speed gearbox from a later MG TC, modified with a Quaife ATB differential and a lightweight aluminum bellhousing by Moss Motors. Fit a custom lightweight flywheel (12 lbs) by Tilton Engineering and a twin-plate ceramic clutch from Spec Clutches, designed for high-torque applications. Replace the original rear axle with a Salisbury-type limited-slip differential from a Jaguar XK120, modified to fit the TA’s chassis with custom mounting brackets by Rimmers Bros.
estetica: Fit a custom widebody kit by British coachbuilder Radford, extending the front and rear fenders by 2 inches to accommodate wider track widths. Use hand-formed aluminum panels for the fenders, paired with a custom front splitter and rear diffuser by Fibreworks, fabricated from carbon fiber for weight reduction and rigidity.

Replace the original steel wheels with a set of 16x6-inch Minilite magnesium wheels, shod with 185/55R16 Avon CR6ZZ tires for period-correct grip. Pair with a set of custom 4-piston aluminum brake calipers by HiSpec Motorsport, designed specifically for the TA’s drum-to-disc conversion, with 280mm vented rotors and braided stainless steel brake lines.

Install a custom interior by Trimcraft, featuring lightweight Recaro SRD bucket seats with 4-point harnesses by Schroth Racing. Replace the original dashboard with a custom aluminum panel by Speedhut, housing a set of Smiths gauges (oil pressure, water temperature, and fuel level) and a Stack digital tachometer. Fit a custom roll cage by Safety Devices, designed to retain the TA’s original roofline while providing structural rigidity for track use.
